data.title: Bowlero rebrands as Lucky Strike Entertainment
data.description:
• As part of its expansion into water parks and family entertainment centers, Bowlero (NYSE:BOWL) is rebranding itself as Lucky Strike Entertainment effective December 12 and will begin trading on the New York Stock Exchange under the new "LUCK" ticker.
  • "As Bowlero Corp. continues to expand into new entertainment verticals, we recognize the need for a brand that reflects the full scope of our offerings. This positions the company to embrace a more diverse range of experiences and firmly establishes our future, furthering our leadership in the entertainment landscape,” CEO Thomas Shannon said in a statement.
  • As part of the rebranding, 75 Bowlero centers will change to Lucky Strike locations within the new two years, along with the debut of a flagship location in Beverly Hills, California.
  • Shares are 2.4% higher on Monday.

data.title: This is how the computer and electronics retail stocks are performing today
data.description:
As cyber-Monday has arrived, here is a look at five computer and electronic retail stocks:

ATRenew Inc. (RERE) – Quant rating: 4.89

Up 3.49% from the past five days, and so far, 69.79% year-to-date.

The stock is “positioned for growth” according to a recent analysis by Ivy Global Insights, after strong third quarter earnings results.

“The company’s robust cash position and innovative AI-powered technologies position it well for future growth and sustainable margins in the circular economy market,” the Ivy Global Insights said.

The company also appears undervalued and trading at a great discount.

GameStop Corp. (GME) – Quant rating: 3.17

GameStop (GME) is down 6.33% in the past five days, but up about 65% year-to-date.

The company’s topline remains weak and its accelerating revenue is declining, said Seeking Alpha analyst Florian Muller in a recent analysis.

“Despite optimistic valuation adjustments, GameStop's per share value still lies significantly below market price, making it a speculative play rather than a sound investment,” he wrote.

Best Buy Co. (BBY) – Quant rating: 3.16

Up 3.66% from the past five days, but so far, only 15.56% year-to-date.

The company just had disappointing third quarter earnings results, missing revenue and EPS targets, with sales growth down 2.9%.

“The challenging macroeconomic environment, election distractions, and uncompetitive business model against Walmart and Amazon are significant headwinds for Best Buy,” said Seeking Alpha analyst Harrison Schwartz in a recent analysis.

He also believes the company will not recover by 2027. “I forecast a long-term decline that may accelerate with economic weakness next year.”

Currys Plc (OTC:DSITF) – Quant rating: 3.00

Currys (OTC:DSITF) is down 3.92% today, although up 46.93% year-to-date.

The company has been in a slow decline since April 2021, after successfully recovering from the pandemic lows.

“Retailers, such as Currys (OTC:DSITF) have produced stunning share price growth over recent months – with the firm’s market valuation rising by 87% in the past year alone,” according to a Questor article by The Telegraph. “Its shares have been particularly popular amongst investors because the company sells products that typically experience large fluctuations in demand, depending on the consumer outlook.”

Smart Share Global Limited (EM) – Quant rating: 2.63

Up 1.72% today, and up 47.7% year-to-date.

The company has increased its revenue by 50% year-over-year, and its operating margin has reached 1.3%.

Seeking Alpha analyst Tony Investing is bullish on the stock.

“I think the company's revenue will continue to show strong growth rates due to the recovery in mobility and the relatively low base of comparison last year,” he wrote recently.

data.title: Yield curve inversion talks return with the US2Y and US10Y both at 4.20%
data.description:
The shorter end U.S. 2 Year Treasury yield (US2Y) and the longer end U.S. 10 Year (US10Y) look to potentially re-invert on Monday as the two instruments both trade at 4.20%.

The 2s10s yield curve initially inverted back on July 5, 2022 and remained inverted for 793 days until it course corrected back on August 5.

Since the curve flipped positive, the even 0.00% level has acted as a technical level of support that is now being re-tested. The 2s10’s curve also hovers right on its 100-day moving average as well.

Furthermore, see what some Seeking Alpha analysts are saying about the status of the yield space:

SA Analyst James Picerno said: “The recent rise in the 10-year yield suggests that bond market is pricing in higher inflation risk.”

SA Analyst Crystal Allen said: “The Fed's recent rate cuts have not lowered 10-year and 2-year Treasury rates, which have recently crossed paths, indicating a potential ‘bear steepening’ and possible recession.”

SA Analyst Infrastructure Capital Advisors said: “Our models show that the Trump administration’s pro-growth policies of lowering taxes on investment and constraining consumption are anti-inflationary. Therefore, inflation fears and the recent rise in bond yields are way overdone.”

Moreover, for traders and investors looking to further track the status of Treasury yields and the fixed income space here are several notable Treasury and fixed income-focused ETFs to keep in mind:

Treasury ETFs: (TLT), (TLH), (IEF), (IEI), (SHY), (SGOV), (SCHO), and (BIL).

Bond ETFs: (AGG), (BND), (VCIT), (MUB), (MBB), (JNK), (LQD), (HYG), and (TIP).

data.title: U.K. seeks to strengthen military supply chain amid 2.5% spending goal
data.description:
The United Kingdom will stress-test the resiliency of its domestic supply chain and military as the government prepares a plan to increase defense spending, Bloomberg News reported Monday.

Defense Secretary John Healey announced plans for a Defense Industrial Strategy that would bring jobs to every nation and region of the UK. The strategy is part of a broader plan to increase yearly defense spending to 2.5% of gross domestic product.

The stress test will study how to keep forces on the front line through a range of scenarios, such as a protracted conflict and various supply chain disruptions.

“Our forces need to be better equipped and behind them needs to be an industry that is better able to produce what they need and innovate at the pace of wartime pressures,” Healey said to Bloomberg TV in London. “I’ve made decisions that are long overdue.”

The government plans to propose the Defense Industrial Strategy in the first half of 2025, with a remit to last until 2035. Investors, industry and unions will have until the end of February to comment on the proposals.

data.title: TRX gold production fell in FY 2024 but gains forecast for 2025
data.description:
TRX Gold (NYSE:TRX) +1.6% in Monday's trading after reporting lower Y/Y gold production for the full year ended August 31, but the company said it expects output to increase next year thanks to expanded operations in Tanzania.

For FY 2024, TRX (TRX) said it poured 19,389 oz of gold, down 6.5% from 20,759 oz production in FY 2023 and in line with revised full-year production guidance, and sold 19,075 gold oz, which resulted in full-year record revenue of $41.2M, up 7.5% Y/Y, and record adjusted EBITDA of $15.3M, up 11.5% Y/Y.

For FY 2025, the company said it expects gold production from its Buckreef project in Tanzania will increase compared to the prior fiscal year, reflecting a full year of operations from the expanded plant that will see an extra 2K metric tons/day processed.

TRX (TRX) said a waste-stripping campaign scheduled for the new fiscal year means it expects gold production will be lower in H1 before increasing in H2.

data.title: Roku and Trade Desk shares rise after analyst highlights benefits of possible merger
data.description:
Guggenheim suggested that a hypothetical merger between The Trade Desk (NASDAQ:TTD) and Roku (NASDAQ:ROKU) could work to each other's strengths and ambitions

TTD and ROKU were up more than 5% in early open market trading on Monday.

The research firm said the relationship between the two can be bolstered via collaboration rather than competition.

They noted that TTD's Ventura OS, which is currently in the works, would face a long climb to achieve the market penetration necessary to impact the industry, and both companies see common threats from Amazon and Alphabet.

"With ROKU shares currently trading at 2x 2025E platform sales, we believe TTD could rapidly scale its OS ambitions via ROKU’s 85mm+ global streaming household footprint, while ROKU could quickly leverage its first-party viewer data and expanding CTV inventory to match with growing advertiser demand," the research firm said Monday.

TTD is up more than 82% while ROKU is down 22% so far this year. The benchmark S&P index is up more than 26%.

data.title: Tesla Holiday Update delivers Apple Watch options, Santa wraps for Cybertruck
data.description:
As part of its Holiday Update, Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) owners will now be able to perform a host of new tasks from their Apple (AAPL) smartwatch or through the Tesla app to find parking spots, incorporate weather delays into navigation, and perform holiday-themed light shows.

The Tesla (TSLA) app on the Apple (AAPL) Watch will allow drivers to use the device as a phone key, see how much battery charge is left, open the “frunk,” and turn on the vehicle’s climate control.

Cybertruck owners can now turn their system avatars into “Santa’s sleigh” or other custom wraps and enable passengers in the rear seats to play arcade games.

Model 3, Model Y and X owners can now program their headlamps into a themed holiday light show through the Holiday Update and amuse their passengers with a “bum detection” option to turn the passenger seat into a "fart on contact" whoopee cushion.

Along with the lighthearted new options, Tesla (TSLA) is also testing its latest full self-driving version, with plans to introduce it to the entire fleet by the end of the year.

FSD V13.2 promises to reduce the number of driver interventions, including a “parked to parked” function. This would enable the vehicle to move from a parked position to its final destination, in addition to performing three-point turns, and even find parking spots without user intervention. This option will be available to all vehicles except the Cybertruck.

Tesla (TSLA) is also making SiriusXM (SIRI) available for the Model 3, Y, and the Cybertruck.

data.title: U.S. puts restrictions on China's access to AI chips, tools
data.description:
Update: Adds China's response

The U.S. Department of Commerce has placed additional curbs on China’s access to vital tools and components for AI chips.

The new rules restrict sales of 27 types of high-bandwidth memory (HBM) and chipmaking equipment, including products made outside the country by American companies, and are in addition to existing curbs affecting advanced logic chips.

The department has also blacklisted an additional 140 Chinese entities that it said have been operating on behalf of the Chinese government. The names of these companies were not given in the initial statement.

The full list of new sanctions and company additions will be detailed later on Monday, a U.S. official told Bloomberg last week.

“This action is the culmination of the Biden-Harris Administration’s targeted approach, in concert with our allies and partners, to impair the PRC’s ability to indigenize the production of advanced technologies that pose a risk to our national security,” Secretary of Commerce Gina Raimondo said in a statement. “No Administration has been tougher in strategically addressing China’s military modernization through export controls.”

The controls unveiled Monday include exemptions for ally countries such as the Netherlands and Japan, according to a senior administration official, which are expected to impose similar restrictions. Neither government has so far issued such additional rules.

South Korea’s SK Hynix (OTCPK:HXSCF), a key Nvidia (NVDA) supplier, is a current leader in providing HBM chips, followed by Micron Technology (MU) and Samsung Electronics (OTCPK:SSNLF). The controls may also likely hurt U.S. chipmakers like Lam Research (LRCX), KLA Corp (KLAC) and Applied Materials (AMAT).

The Chinese Ministry of Commerce has called the U.S. restrictions “economic coercion,” and “seriously” threatening to the global supply chain. It said that the country will take measures to safeguard its interests, but did not disclose what these measures would be.

data.title: Sana gets FDA Fast Track designation for SC291 for lupus
data.description:
• Sana Biotechnology (NASDAQ:SANA) has received Fast Track designation for its cell therapy SC291 for the treatment of relapsed/refractory systemic lupus erythematosus, or SLE, which includes extrarenal lupus and lupus nephritis.
  • The company is currently enrolling patients in a clinical trial for SC291 in the treatment of B-cell mediated autoimmune diseases, including SLE. Initial data is expected next year, according to a statement.

data.title: Amazon partners with startup to pilot AI tech for data center carbon removal
data.description:
Amazon Web Services (NASDAQ:AMZN) has partnered with startup Orbital Materials to utilize its artificial intelligence platform to develop advanced materials capable of reducing the carbon footprint of data centers.

The proliferation of AI has led to a spike in data center electricity demand, which is in turn has created the risks of increased emissions by cloud providers such as AWS. Ironically, AWS and Orbital are attempting to use AI to minimize these carbon emissions.

A study by the International Energy Agency that data centers now account for 2% to 4% of total electricity consumption in the U.S., and account for as much as 10% in several states.

AWS plans to work with Orbital to synthesize and test new technologies and materials for data center carbon renewal, chip cooling and water utilization. Orbital is expected to pilot this technology by the end of 2025.

"Since establishing its lab in the first quarter of 2024, Orbital has achieved a 10x improvement in its material's performance through the use of its AI platform - an order of magnitude faster than traditional development and breaking new ground in carbon removal efficacy," according to AWS.

"Working with the market-leading AWS team will ensure that our suite of products in cooling, water utilization and carbon removal enables the next generation of data centers powering the AI revolution," said Orbital CEO and co-founder Jonathan Godwin.

Orbital will also offer its open-source AI model for simulating advanced materials through Amazon SageMaker JumpStart and AWS Marketplace.

"Together, we have the opportunity to set new benchmarks for carbon removal and efficiency across the industry," said Howard Gefen, General Manager of AWS Energy & Utilities.

AWS is holding its re:Invent 2024 conference this week. The event focuses on the global cloud-computing community. It takes place in Las Vegas.

Amazon shares edged up 2% during Monday morning trading.
